articles.title: Could Patriots reunite with Stefon Diggs? One Patriots writer doesn’t expect it.
articles.description:
"That [post] sounded like, Hey, I'm open for business, right? Like — ‘Anybody want me?’ kind of thing."

The post Could Patriots reunite with Stefon Diggs? One Patriots writer doesn’t expect it. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Patriots writer doubts Stefon Diggs will join New England this offseason.
Diggs hinted at being open to offers on social media recently.
Potential reunion could affect Patriots' offensive strategy and fan engagement.

articles.title: Man, 31, killed in New Bedford shooting
articles.description:
Steven Coan, 31, was pronounced dead at a New Bedford hospital after suffering multiple gunshot wounds inside his car, the DA said.

The post Man, 31, killed in New Bedford shooting appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Steven Coan, 31, was shot and killed in New Bedford on May 11.
He suffered multiple gunshot wounds inside his car before being pronounced dead at a hospital.
This incident raises concerns about gun violence and safety in nearby Boston communities.

articles.title: South Florida officers sue Ben Affleck and Matt Damon, claiming details in ‘The Rip’ are too real
articles.description:
“The Rip” features Affleck and Damon as South Florida police officers who find millions of dollars inside a house. Parts of the movie were inspired by a real 2016 case.

The post South Florida officers sue Ben Affleck and Matt Damon, claiming details in ‘The Rip’ are too real appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: South Florida officers filed a lawsuit against Ben Affleck and Matt Damon over 'The Rip'.
The film is inspired by a 2016 case involving millions found in a house.
Boston residents may see increased scrutiny on film portrayals of real-life events.

articles.title: Hayden, Rollins, and third Suffolk DA candidate Champion secure enough signatures to appear on ballot
articles.description:
Kevin Hayden, Rachael Rollins, and Linda Champion have cleared a crucial threshold, according to data from local officials. It sets the stage for a three-way primary race.

The post Hayden, Rollins, and third Suffolk DA candidate Champion secure enough signatures to appear on ballot appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kevin Hayden, Rachael Rollins, and Linda Champion qualify for Suffolk DA primary ballot.
Candidates secured enough signatures as confirmed by local officials on May 11, 2026.
Boston residents will vote in a competitive three-way primary for district attorney.
